en.extract:
The western tinkerbird (Pogoniulus coryphaea) is a species of bird in the family Lybiidae native to Central Africa, where it has been recorded at altitudes from 900 to 3,030 m (2,950 to 9,940 ft).
It has a threefold area of presence : the Angolan montane forest–grassland mosaic, the Western High Plateau and the Albertine Rift montane forests.

The '''western tinkerbird''' ('''''Pogoniulus coryphaea''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Lybiidae]] native to [[Central Africa]], where it has been recorded at altitudes from {{convert|900|to|3030|m|ft|abbr=on}}.<ref name=iucn/>

It has a threefold area of presence : the [[Angolan montane forest–grassland mosaic]], the [[Western High Plateau]] and the [[Albertine Rift montane forests]].
